data.title: Create scale-inference.md shared prompt for deterministic scale signal inference
data.description:
## Testing Mode
RED (implementation half — Task 651c-652f created the RED test; this task makes assertions 1-5 pass; assertion 6 for brainstorm reference still fails until Task 5a)

## What to Build
Create `plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/scale-inference.md`.

## Required Sections and Content

### ## Scale Signal Sources
A table of artifacts to check before web search or user questions:

| Artifact | What to Look For |
|----------|------------------|
| `.claude/design-notes.md` | Explicit volume statements, domain context, deployment scope |
| `dso-config.conf` | Config keys implying scale (max_agents, rate limits, queue sizes, TTLs) |
| `workflow-config.yaml` / `pyproject.toml` | Dependency choices that imply scale (caching libs, async frameworks) |
| Project README or docs | Stated user population, record counts, request rates |

### ## Inference Protocol
Document the 3-step deterministic protocol (must execute in order, no skipping):
- Step 1: Check all artifacts in Scale Signal Sources table. Extract any numeric volume signal or explicit scope statement.
- Step 2: If no usable estimate found in artifacts, perform a domain web search using domain terms from the ticket description and design-notes.md (e.g., "NJ unemployment claims annual volume").
- Step 3: If Step 2 yields no usable estimate, ask the user one targeted question: "What is the expected [metric] volume for this feature?"

### ## Usable Estimate Definition
A usable estimate is:
- A numeric order-of-magnitude value (e.g., "~10K records/day", "millions of requests/hour"), OR
- An explicit statement that volume is negligible or unbounded (e.g., "config file read once at startup", "internal tool, 5-10 users")

If neither is found after Steps 1 and 2, proceed to Step 3.

### ## Default Assumption
When no usable estimate is available and the user is not reachable (non-interactive mode): **assume small scale unless evidence found**. Do not apply performance optimizations, caching layers, or high-throughput architectures without a scale estimate.

### ## Prohibition on Upward Interpolation
Never assume a higher scale than the evidence supports. If the only evidence is "this is a government portal", do not extrapolate to "millions of users" — look up the specific agency's reported statistics or ask. Upward interpolation without evidence is prohibited regardless of the domain's apparent size.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/scale-inference.md` exists and is non-empty
  Verify: `test -s plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/scale-inference.md`
- [ ] Has "Scale Signal Sources" section with artifact table
  Verify: `grep -q "Scale Signal Sources" plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/scale-inference.md`
- [ ] Has "Inference Protocol" section with 3-step structure
  Verify: `grep -q "Inference Protocol" plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/scale-inference.md && grep -q "Step 1" plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/scale-inference.md`
- [ ] Has "Default Assumption" section stating small-scale default
  Verify: `grep -q "Default Assumption" plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/scale-inference.md`
- [ ] Has prohibition on upward interpolation
  Verify: `grep -qi "upward interpolation\|Upward Interpolation" plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/scale-inference.md`
- [ ] `bash tests/skills/test-scale-inference-shared-prompt.sh` file-existence and structure sections pass; brainstorm-reference section still fails (expected — RED marker remains)
  Verify: `bash tests/skills/test-scale-inference-shared-prompt.sh 2>&1 | grep -c "PASS"` — expect 5+ passes
- [ ] `plugins/dso/scripts/validate.sh --ci` passes
  Verify: `plugins/dso/scripts/validate.sh --ci`
